growth_records.medium: Not stated (genome-scale FBA model case study; glucose as sole carbon substrate)

up.units: mmol/g DCW/h (fixed inflow flux / FBA model constraint)
up.method: FBA model constraint (not measured in this paper)

sec.units: mmol/g DCW/h (baseline FBA scenario; reduced to 0.6 to boost ethanol in silico)
predicted (trade-off vs growth rate explored, Figure 5)

mfa.note: Fixed model constraint for X514 FBA
Baseline FBA scenario; inhibiting to 0.6 increased in-silico ethanol
Fitted so FBA growth matches experimental mu=0.042/h; higher than C. thermocellum 150

yields.note: Trade-off between ethanol production and biomass growth predicted (Figure 5); no measured yield

growth_records.cite: Feng, Xueyang et al. MicrobesFlux: a web platform for drafting metabolic models from the KEGG database. BMC Systems Biology (2012). DOI:10.1186/1752-0509-6-94. [PMC3447728]
growth_records.method: reported - cited from external ref [29]; used as experimental target to fit GAM; doubling time calculated (ln2/0.042)
growth_records.snippet: ...glucose inflow flux was fixed as 3.92 mmol/g DCW/h... NGAM was chosen as 7.6 mmol ATP/g DCW/h... found the value of GAM that could match the experimental measurement (i.e. growth rate of strain X514 as 0.042 h-1 reported in [29]) to be 2
growth_records.notes: Modeling/tool paper (MicrobesFlux); Thermoanaerobacter sp. X514 FBA case study. The growth rate mu=0.042/h is CITED FROM external ref [29] (not measured here); doubling_time = ln2/0.042 = 16.50 h. Glucose uptake 3.92 mmol/gDCW/h is a FIXED FBA CONSTRAINT (is_experimental=false). Acetate 2.0 and ethanol are FBA simulation outputs. NGAM 7.6 mmol ATP/gDCW/h from ref [32]; GAM 220 mmol ATP/gDCW fitted. No wet-lab cultivation, no medium composition. Included for MFA/model-flux completeness only.
